what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-spec for the view F1, a desktop/mobile top-bar/cockpit surface in area F; it lists the UI addresses the spec was built against, the components it touches, and the build-time lint and token sources it depends on.
observations: journeys and cross_journeys are empty arrays even though addresses are listed
Represents three concerns: RHM-modes, RUN-run, INTRO-cap
Contains 18 ui:// scheme addresses spanning activity, canvas, chat, chrome, inbox, settings, studio, tabbar, and toolbar
build_to includes a components list, a design_lint shell command, and a tokens-to-CSS mapping
standouts: journeys[] and cross_journeys[] are empty despite the file describing an address web
The directory path '_unrouted' matches the unrouted home_journey status
build_to embeds a lint command and a token build artifact in the surface spec
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to
Uses a ui:// address namespace for UI locations
Maps design tokens from tokens.json to a design-system.css output
concerns: Empty journeys/cross_journeys with a populated addresses list suggests routing is incomplete
The design_lint command references external paths and a 'fail-on' mode not defined in this file
No explicit mapping from individual addresses to components or journeys
Status 'planned' indicates this is not yet realized
notes: A planned cockpit surface spec that enumerates target UI addresses and downstream build artifacts but has not yet been assigned journey routes.
questions: Why are journeys and cross_journeys empty when addresses are already enumerated?
What do RHM-modes, RUN-run, and INTRO-cap specifically denote?
How do the 18 addresses map to the 9 listed components?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface-spec for view F1; it matches, except the journey routing layer is still unresolved.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a top-bar/cockpit surface by enumerating every UI address and component it must coordinate, while deferring journey routing to a later design step.
novelty: It embeds build-time tooling metadata (lint command and token source) directly into a design surface spec, and records the exact address set used during generation.
contribution: A structured surface record that names the view, its area, platforms, represented concerns, target UI addresses, affected components, and the build checks and token artifacts needed to realize it.
summary_for_embedding: A planned JSON surface-spec for view F1, a desktop/mobile top-bar/cockpit in area F, listing represented concerns, 18 ui:// addresses, nine affected components, and build-time lint and token dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the scope and build targets of the F1 cockpit surface before its journey routes are resolved.
